name: Ariel University International Program
durationText: 1 semester up to a full year
goodFor: For gap-year or semester students who want English-taught university courses fully integrated with Israeli dorm-mates on Ariel's campus, paired with an intensive 500-hour Hebrew ulpan.

name: Jerusalem Academy of Music and Dance – DanceJerusalem
organization: Jerusalem Academy of Music and Dance, in partnership with Hebrew University's Rothberg International School
goodFor: For serious international dance students who want university-level ballet/contemporary training (including Gaga) combined with Hebrew University coursework on Israeli history and society; an audition/portfolio is required.

name: Weizmann Institute – Kupcinet-Getz International Summer School
goodFor: For top-performing science/math undergraduates (3.6+ GPA) who want a highly competitive 7-week hands-on research placement in a Weizmann lab under a scientist's supervision, not a touring or cultural-immersion program.

name: Musrara International Residency Program
organization: Musrara – The Naggar School of Art and Society
goodFor: For juried interdisciplinary artists (photography, new media, video, sound) wanting an intensive two-week international residency in Jerusalem alongside Israeli artists, or its separate multi-year photography/new-media degree tracks.

name: Sam Spiegel Jerusalem International Film Lab
organization: Sam Spiegel Film and Television School
durationText: Multi-session lab spread across roughly a year; degree tracks are 2-4 years
goodFor: For a small, highly selective cohort (12 filmmakers) of local and international emerging filmmakers developing a debut feature film in Jerusalem, rather than a broad study-abroad experience.

name: Birthright Israel Excel Fellowship
durationText: 10-week summer internship, plus multi-year fellowship community
goodFor: For high-achieving Jewish college students (3.2+ GPA) who want an elite, highly competitive 10-week Tel Aviv internship at a top firm or startup plus a lifelong 1,500+ alumni professional network.

name: Tech2Peace
location: Jerusalem area and West Bank sites (Al Quds, Abu Dis, Jericho)
goodFor: For Israelis and Palestinians ages 20-30 who want an intensive 10-day mix of tech/entrepreneurship training and facilitated conflict-dialogue in a highly competitive cohort (about 60 spots per ~500 applicants).
note: Description doesn't confirm whether diaspora Anglos join, only Israelis+Palestinians — angloMix left blank rather than guessed.

name: Big Idea Gap Year
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting Israel's only hi-tech-focused year — a 4-month coding bootcamp plus internship at Be'er Sheva companies like Wix and PayPal — over a Torah-study or touring-focused year; Masa grants may reduce cost.

name: Torah Tech
organization: Torah Tech (an Am Yisrael Foundation initiative)
goodFor: For young Orthodox men who want to pair serious Beit Midrash learning with real Tel Aviv startup internships, preparing for both a Torah-centered life and a tech career.

name: Project TEN (Kibbutz Harduf)
organization: Project TEN / The Jewish Agency for Israel
goodFor: For young adults who want grassroots coexistence and disability-support volunteering on a Galilee kibbutz alongside pluralistic Jewish learning and Hebrew/Arabic classes; Masa grants may be available.

name: Save a Child's Heart Volunteer Program
location: Holon (Wolfson Medical Center / Sylvan Adams Children's Hospital)
cost: Free to volunteer; participants arrange and cover their own accommodations
goodFor: For volunteers 18+ who want to support children from developing countries receiving pediatric cardiac care in Holon, with commitments from a 3-week 'Immersive' track up to longer 'Superstar'/'Buddy' placements.

name: Leket Israel Volunteer Program
organization: Leket Israel — The National Food Bank
location: Nationwide (fields, packing centers and partner NPOs across Israel)
durationText: Flexible — single sessions up to ongoing/recurring volunteering
cost: Free
goodFor: For anyone wanting flexible, no-experience-needed volunteering — gleaning produce, night food-rescue runs, or sandwich-packing — with Israel's national food-rescue nonprofit, from single sessions to ongoing involvement.
note: Runs nationwide at flexible sites rather than one campus or a touring circuit — travelType left blank.

name: Hadassah Medical Center Volunteer Program
location: Jerusalem (Ein Kerem and Mount Scopus campuses)
goodFor: For short-term volunteers (1-3 months) who want hands-on placement alongside nursing staff or in patient-care roles at Hadassah's two Jerusalem university hospitals.

name: Mechinat Lachish
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
location: Judean foothills — campuses at Nahal Oz, Eliav and Kibbutz Beit Guvrin
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina in the Judean foothills that pairs standard Jewish-identity/Zionism study with coursework in the arts and environmentalism.

name: Mechinat Telem
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting an urban, pluralistic co-ed mechina centered on social-justice and coexistence work within Tel Aviv-Jaffa's diverse Jewish and Arab communities.

name: Mechinat HaEmek
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
location: Kfar Rupin Kibbutz, near the Sea of Galilee
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina on a Sea-of-Galilee-area kibbutz with an emphasis on nature exploration and bridging different sectors of Israeli society.

name: Mechinat Meitzar
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina on the Golan Heights focused on initiative, responsibility and leadership toward building a democratic Jewish society.

name: Mechinat Five Fingers (Chamesh Etzbaot)
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina (est. 2018) built around a distinctive physical-and-mental resilience training system in Zichron Ya'akov.

name: Mechinat Melach HaAretz
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
location: Dead Sea region — Ein Gedi (girls) and Mitzpe Shalem (boys)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting the standard mechina curriculum set in the Dead Sea region, split across two single-gender campuses (Ein Gedi for women, Mitzpe Shalem for men) rather than one co-ed site.

name: Mechinat Aderet
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
location: Ayanot, Ben Shemen and Kfar Silver
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina across three campuses emphasizing social and environmental responsibility in a new generation of Israeli leadership.

name: Mechinat Amichai
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students of any religious background wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina in Beer Sheva centered on community volunteering in the Negev.

name: Mechinat Hannaton
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina in the Yizrael Valley centered on exploring Jewish-Israeli identity through study, leadership training and volunteer work.

name: Mechinat Rakia
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed leadership mechina in Harish that draws on Israeli Air Force heritage and values as its training framework.

name: Mechinat Arava
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
location: Hatzeva, Paran, Zofar and Ein-Yahav (Arava Valley)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina deliberately open to all Jewish denominations, spread across several small Arava Valley communities.

name: Mechinat Be'eri
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina emphasizing progressive and Zionist leadership development alongside community volunteering.

name: Mechinat Dror
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a secular/pluralistic co-ed mechina in the western Negev near the Gaza border, with community volunteering in the region.

name: Mechinat Tavor
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
location: Northern Israel — Nof HaGalil, Ramat HaShofet and Kiryat Shmona
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students of any religious background wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina spanning several northern Israel communities.

name: Mechinat Haviva Reich
organization: The Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies (Yachad/JCM)
cost: $14,000-$16,000 (covers education, room, board, transit, insurance and laundry)
goodFor: For gap-year students wanting a pluralistic co-ed mechina in northern Israel named for WWII Jewish parachutist Haviva Reich, combining study, volunteering and IDF-service preparation.

name: Kadima (World Bnei Akiva)
cost: $17,000-$25,000, with $5,000-$10,000 Masa grants typically available
goodFor: For religious-Zionist gap-year students wanting a broad taste of Israeli life — internships, kibbutz living, volunteering, MDA training, a taste of Marva IDF prep, seminars and travel — rather than one fixed campus.

name: Mechina Olamit (World Bnei Akiva)
cost: $17,000-$25,000, with $5,000-$10,000 Masa grants typically available
goodFor: For religious-Zionist gap-year students wanting a 9.5-month leadership program with shared living and hands-on leadership experience alongside Israeli peers at World Bnei Akiva's flagship international campus near Jerusalem.

name: Torani (World Bnei Akiva)
durationText: Full gap year, run alongside a yeshiva/seminary program
goodFor: For students already enrolled in a separate yeshiva or midrasha gap year who want to layer on additional leadership training, seminars, Shabbatonim, a kibbutz stay and a wider Israel-wide Bnei Akiva peer community.
note: An add-on layered onto a separate single-gender yeshiva/seminary enrollment — gender and travelType depend on the base program, left blank rather than guessed.

name: MTA (World Bnei Akiva)
goodFor: For religious-Zionist gap-year students specifically from Australia, New Zealand or South Africa wanting immersive Beit Midrash learning and Torat Eretz Yisrael at one of four campuses.

name: Ulpan Etzion
location: Jerusalem, Haifa, Ra'anana, Ramle, Be'er Sheva and Kibbutz Tzuba
durationText: 5 months (an optional 3-month Etzion Plus extension is available at the Ra'anana campus)
goodFor: For young-adult olim and prospective immigrants (22-35, college degree) wanting the Jewish Agency's flagship 5-month intensive Hebrew ulpan (5 hrs/day, 5 days/week), with prep for IDF service or Israeli employment.
note: Built for immigrants becoming Israeli, not diaspora Anglos studying alongside natives — israeli-anglo-mix tag doesn't cleanly apply.

name: Kibbutz Ulpan
location: Ein Hashofet, Ma'agan Michael, Sde Eliyahu and Etzion Tzuba kibbutzim
goodFor: For tourists and prospective immigrants wanting a 5-month immersive rural experience — kibbutz dorm living and work assignments paired with multi-level Hebrew classes; Masa grants may be available.

name: Ulpan-Or Sabra Immersion Program
durationText: 10 days to several weeks, fully personalized
goodFor: For anyone wanting fully personalized, one-on-one Hebrew instruction (in Jerusalem or worldwide via Zoom) using the 'Rapid Language Acquisition' method, from a 10-day intensive up to several weeks.

name: Neve Yerushalayim – Shalhevet Program
goodFor: For women, including complete beginners, who want a foundational year building Jewish knowledge and spiritual growth at one of the world's largest women's seminaries (35,000+ alumni since 1970).

name: Neve Yerushalayim – Machon Devorah
goodFor: For post-high-school and graduate-age women wanting an alternative seminary track within the broader Neve Yerushalayim network on the same Har Nof campus.

name: Ohr Somayach – Derech Institute for Torah Studies
durationText: 1 year (customized programs also available for South African and Australian post-high-school students)
goodFor: For motivated post-high-school men without a strong Jewish educational background who want to build independent Talmud/text-study skills from the ground up.

name: Aish HaTorah – Aish Gesher
goodFor: For young men from public-school backgrounds wanting a traditional year-long Old City yeshiva experience combining serious text study with leadership development.

name: Aish HaTorah – Foundations Program
goodFor: For men on a gap year or semester break wanting a flexible-length (3 weeks-3 months), no-background-assumed introduction to Jewish philosophy, history and text study in the Old City.

name: Shapell's / Yeshiva Darché Noam
organization: David Shapell College of Jewish Studies
goodFor: For English-speaking college graduates and young professionals (20s-30s) wanting flexible-length Torah study (1-3 months up to 6+ months) in Gemara, Chumash, Halacha and Hashkafa alongside character development.

name: Machon Shlomo
cost: $15,500 per year (tuition, room and board)
goodFor: For English-speaking Jewish college graduates and young professionals (20s-30s) pausing careers for 1-2 years of Torah learning, personal development and communal life in Har Nof.

name: Beit Chana Seminary (Tzfat) – Chul Program
goodFor: For overseas young women with strong Hebrew proficiency wanting Chabad-Chassidic teacher training — leading to a government-recognized 'Morot Lagolah' certificate — at one of Lubavitch's most sought-after seminaries.

name: Diaspora Yeshiva – Gap Year Program
durationText: A year or less (including the optional B.A. track)
cost: 5,600 NIS (~$1,800) per month for yeshiva tuition, room and board; approximately $12,000 additional for the B.A. track; tuition subsidies available on request
goodFor: For male high-school graduates wanting a gap year of Torah/Talmud study tailored to their level on Mount Zion, with the option to simultaneously earn an accredited B.A. within the year; tuition subsidies available on request.

name: Yeshivat Sha'arei Mevaseret Zion
durationText: 1 year, with a common second-year (Shana Bet) option
goodFor: For post-high-school boys from North America, the UK, South America and Australia wanting Gemara study across all levels alongside Mussar/Hashkafa/Chassidus/Tanach, with most students returning for an official Shana Bet second year.

name: Yeshivas Bircas HaTorah
durationText: Flexible: from several weeks up to a full year or more
goodFor: For learners of any age and Jewish background wanting flexible-length Torah study — from short-term visitor programs up to a full year or more — at a yeshiva founded in 1989 in the Old City's Jewish Quarter.

name: Midreshet AMIT
goodFor: For women wanting a Religious-Zionist year combining Torah learning with hands-on chesed, becoming part of the extended foster family for the children of Beit Hayeled.

name: Michlalah Jerusalem College – Machal (Overseas Students Program)
goodFor: For roughly 140 international women per year wanting a Torah-growth curriculum (Tanach, Halacha, Mussar, Machshava) focused on personal commitment to Torah life, within a college founded to train Israeli teachers.
